tools.name: setup
tools.description: Detect OS, check available tools, and install required Python packages. Always run first. Returns OS info and tool availability status.
tools.bash:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
OS=$(uname -s)
PKG_MGR=""

if [ "$OS" = "Darwin" ]; then
  PKG_MGR="brew"
  echo "[os] macOS"
elif [ "$OS" = "Linux" ]; then
  if [ -f /etc/debian_version ]; then
    PKG_MGR="apt"
    echo "[os] Ubuntu/Debian $(cat /etc/debian_version 2>/dev/null)"
  elif [ -f /etc/redhat-release ]; then
    PKG_MGR="yum"
    echo "[os] $(cat /etc/redhat-release)"
  elif [ -f /etc/arch-release ]; then
    PKG_MGR="pacman"
    echo "[os] Arch Linux"
  else
    echo "[os] Linux ($(uname -r))"
  fi
else
  echo "[os] $OS"
fi

python3 -m pip install --quiet --user requests beautifulsoup4 dnspython 2>&1 | grep -v 'already satisfied' | grep -v '^$' || true
echo "[ok] python deps: requests beautifulsoup4 dnspython"

if command -v curl &>/dev/null; then
  echo "[ok] curl $(curl --version | head -1 | awk '{print $2}')"
else
  echo "[warn] curl not found"
  case "$PKG_MGR" in
    brew)    echo "      fix: brew install curl" ;;
    apt)     echo "      fix: sudo apt-get install -y curl" ;;
    yum)     echo "      fix: sudo yum install -y curl" ;;
    pacman)  echo "      fix: sudo pacman -S curl" ;;
  esac
fi

if command -v npx &>/dev/null; then
  echo "[ok] npx $(npx --version 2>/dev/null) — retire.js available"
else
  echo "[skip] npx not found — Step 4b (retire.js) will be skipped"
  case "$PKG_MGR" in
    brew)    echo "       fix: brew install node" ;;
    apt)     echo "       fix: sudo apt-get install -y nodejs npm" ;;
    yum)     echo "       fix: sudo yum install -y nodejs npm" ;;
    pacman)  echo "       fix: sudo pacman -S nodejs npm" ;;
  esac
fi

if command -v python3 &>/dev/null; then
  echo "[ok] python3 $(python3 --version 2>&1 | awk '{print $2}')"
else
  echo "[error] python3 not found"
  case "$PKG_MGR" in
    brew)    echo "        fix: brew install python3" ;;
    apt)     echo "        fix: sudo apt-get install -y python3 python3-pip" ;;
    yum)     echo "        fix: sudo yum install -y python3" ;;
    pacman)  echo "        fix: sudo pacman -S python" ;;
  esac
fi

tools.name: fetch_headers
tools.description: Fetch HTTP response headers from a URL.

tools.name: fetch_page
tools.description: Fetch the full HTML body of a URL.

tools.name: probe_paths
tools.description: Probe common sensitive paths on the target and return HTTP status codes.

base_url.description: Base URL without trailing slash, e.g. https://www.acb.com

tools.bash: for path in /.env /.env.local /.env.production /.git/config /.git/HEAD /robots.txt /sitemap.xml /.well-known/security.txt /swagger.json /swagger-ui.html /openapi.json /graphql /graphiql /api/v1 /api/v2 /admin /admin/login /wp-admin /phpinfo.php /server-status /health /metrics /version /actuator /actuator/env /actuator/health; do status=$(curl -so /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --max-time 5 --user-agent 'Mozilla/5.0' "{{base_url}}$path"); echo "$status {{base_url}}$path"; done

tools.name: fetch_js
tools.description: Download a JavaScript file and return its content.

tools.name: retire_scan
tools.description: Download all JS files from a page into a temp directory and run retire.js to detect vulnerable JavaScript libraries. Requires npx (Node.js). Returns retire.js JSON output.
tools.bash:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
if ! command -v npx &>/dev/null; then
  echo '{"error": "npx not available", "message": "Install Node.js to enable retire.js scanning"}'
  exit 0
fi

TMPDIR=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$TMPDIR"' EXIT

HTML=$(curl -sL --max-time 15 --user-agent 'Mozilla/5.0' '{{base_url}}')

count=0
while IFS= read -r src; do
  [ -z "$src" ] && continue
  if [[ "$src" == http* ]]; then
    url="$src"
  elif [[ "$src" == //* ]]; then
    url="https:$src"
  elif [[ "$src" == /* ]]; then
    url="{{origin}}$src"
  else
    url="{{origin}}/$src"
  fi
  curl -sL --max-time 10 --user-agent 'Mozilla/5.0' "$url" -o "$TMPDIR/${count}.js" 2>/dev/null
  count=$((count + 1))
  [ $count -ge 20 ] && break
done < <(echo "$HTML" | grep -oE 'src="[^"]*\.js[^"]*"' | sed 's/src="//;s/"$//')

if [ $count -eq 0 ]; then
  echo '{"findings": [], "message": "no JS files downloaded"}'
  exit 0
fi

npx --yes retire --js --outputformat json --path "$TMPDIR" 2>/dev/null || echo '{"error": "retire scan failed"}'

tools.name: dns_subdomains
tools.description: Query crt.sh certificate transparency logs for subdomains of a domain.
tools.bash: curl -s --max-time 15 'https://crt.sh/?q=%25.{{domain}}&output=json' | python3 -c "import sys,json; data=json.load(sys.stdin); names=sorted(set(n.strip().lstrip('*.') for e in data for n in e.get('name_value','').splitlines())); print('\n'.join(names))" 2>/dev/null || echo 'crt.sh unavailable'

tools.name: cors_probe
tools.description: Test CORS behaviour by sending a request with an attacker-controlled Origin header.

origin.description: Attacker origin to inject, e.g. https://evil.com
